artPrompt: A vast ghostly hourglass tips backward as an auditor's gloved hand reaches into a swirling vortex of spent dice and coins, plucking one from history while the rest unwind into cyan-lit smoke. Reality folds in concentric ripples down a beige bureaucratic corridor toward a hazy vanishing point. Epic, dizzying film-still composition of time being rewritten, rendered as gritty cinematic digital concept art — highly detailed, dramatic photoreal lighting, atmospheric haze, realistic textures.
name: Retroactive Rule Change, Effective Last Tuesday
text:
Pick any one die roll or coin flip that has happened so far this game — yes, any of them, going back to turn one. It is now retroactively ruled invalid. Undo everything that resulted from it as best the table can remember. Argue. The auditor (you) has final say.
If nobody can remember the roll, it never happened, which was the point all along.
flavorText: "That rule was always in effect. You simply weren't told."
